html:
Exclusive to:

The Particulate Purifier is a Dress item introduced in Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. It requires an Alchemy Engine to prototype, and 2 Empty Bottles, 3 Coral and 1 Jellyfish to craft. The Sewing Kit will repair 50% durability of this item.

When worn, it protects the player character from getting Poisoned by gas cloud attacks of Poisonous Holes and Stink Rays. However, it offers no protection against Poisoning via physical attacks of Poison Snakes, Poison Mosquitoes, or Spider Warriors.

Contents

  • 1 Downloadable Content
  • 2 Tips
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, the Particulate Purifier will grant immunity to the toxic gas in the Gas Rainforest, as well as the Noxious Cloud created by using the Bug B'Gone. It also grants protection against the effects of Hay Fever.

Tips

  • WX-78 is immune to poison, therefore wearing a Particulate Purifier gives no benefits outside of Hamlet.
  • The Particulate Purifier renders the Stink Rays completely harmless, making Stink Ray spawns a very good source of Monster Meat and Venom Glands.

Trivia

Texture of the Gas Mask.
  • Prior to the "Seas The Day!" update, this item was simply called "The Gas Mask", which functioned identically, except for a slightly different recipe and texture. Its description was "Why smell bad smells?", and it cost 2 Empty Bottles, 1 Cloth, and 1 Jellyfish.
    • The Gas Mask also had a voice filter which would increase the pitch of a character's voice when saying something.
    • The concept of the Gas Mask is reused in HAM, bearing a large visual resemblance as well as sharing the same name.

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Willow wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wendy wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • WX-78 wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Woodie wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wes wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Maxwell wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Webber wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Walani wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Warly wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wilbur wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wilba wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wormwood wearing a Particulate Purifier.
  • Wheeler wearing a Particulate Purifier.

The Particulate Purifier is a Dress item introduced in Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. It requires an Alchemy Engine to prototype, and 2 Empty Bottles, 3 Coral and 1 Jellyfish to craft. The Sewing Kit will repair 50% durability of this item.
When worn, it protects the player character from getting Poisoned by gas cloud attacks of Poisonous Holes and Stink Rays. However, it offers no protection against Poisoning via physical attacks of Poison Snakes, Poison Mosquitoes, or Spider Warriors.

1 Downloadable Content
2 Tips
3 Trivia
4 Gallery
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Hamlet DLC, the Particulate Purifier will grant immunity to the toxic gas in the Gas Rainforest, as well as the Noxious Cloud created by using the Bug B'Gone. It also grants protection against the effects of Hay Fever.
Tips[edit | edit source]
WX-78 is immune to poison, therefore wearing a Particulate Purifier gives no benefits outside of Hamlet.
The Particulate Purifier renders the Stink Rays completely harmless, making Stink Ray spawns a very good source of Monster Meat and Venom Glands.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Texture of the Gas Mask.
Prior to the "Seas The Day!" update, this item was simply called "The Gas Mask", which functioned identically, except for a slightly different recipe and texture. Its description was "Why smell bad smells?", and it cost 2 Empty Bottles, 1 Cloth, and 1 Jellyfish.
The Gas Mask also had a voice filter which would increase the pitch of a character's voice when saying something.
The concept of the Gas Mask is reused in HAM, bearing a large visual resemblance as well as sharing the same name.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Máy Lọc Hạt là một Vật phẩm Trang Phục được giới thiệu trong Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. Nó yêu cầu Máy luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu và 2 Chai rỗng, 3 San hô và 1 Sứa để chế tạo. Bộ May vá sẽ sửa chữa độ bền của vật phẩm này 50%.

The '''Particulate Purifier''' is a [[Dress]] [[item]] introduced in ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It requires an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype, and 2 [[Empty Bottles]], 3 [[Coral]] and 1 [[Jellyfish]] to craft. The [[Sewing Kit]] will repair 50% durability of this item.

When worn, it protects the player [[character]] from getting [[Poison|Poisoned]] by gas cloud attacks of [[Poisonous Hole|Poisonous Holes]] and [[Stink Ray|Stink Rays]]. However, it offers no protection against Poisoning via physical attacks of [[Poison Snake|Poison Snakes]], [[Poison Mosquito|Poison Mosquitoes]], or [[Spider/Warrior|Spider Warriors]].

=={{Pic|32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, the Particulate Purifier will grant immunity to the toxic gas in the [[Gas Rainforest]], as well as the Noxious Cloud created by using the [[Bug B'Gone]]. It also grants protection against the effects of [[Hay Fever]].

=={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* [[WX-78]] is immune to poison, therefore wearing a Particulate Purifier gives no benefits outside of Hamlet.
* The Particulate Purifier renders the Stink Rays completely harmless, making Stink Ray spawns a very good source of [[Monster Meat]] and [[Venom Gland|Venom Glands]].

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
[[File:Gas Mask Old.png|thumb|right|Texture of the Gas Mask.]]
* Prior to the ''[https://steamcommunity.com/games/219740/announcements/detail/618490373957391433 "Seas The Day!"]'' update, this item was simply called "The Gas Mask", which functioned identically, except for a slightly different recipe and texture. Its description was "''Why smell bad smells?''", and it cost 2 [[Empty Bottle]]s, 1 [[Cloth]], and 1 [[Jellyfish]].
** The Gas Mask also had a voice filter which would increase the pitch of a character's voice when saying something.
** The concept of the [[Gas Mask]] is reused in HAM, bearing a large visual resemblance as well as sharing the same name.
{{clear}}
